title: (No AI) Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_C_96d15c.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Anthropic just [announced](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5) Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available.

According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision," with its lead over other models growing as tasks become longer and more complex.

Fable 5 marks the first broad release from Anthropic's Mythos class of AI models, after the company said the family was so capable at cybersecurity tasks that it was [too dangerous to release publicly](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917644/anthropic-claude-mythos-breach-humiliation). Anthropic said the release was "made possible by new safeguards that block responses in specific high-risk areas," with …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/news/946725/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-mythos)

According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision," with its lead over other models growing as tasks become longer and more complex.
Fable 5 marks the first broad release from Anthropic's Mythos class of AI models, after the company said the family was so capable at cybersecurity tasks that it was too dangerous to release publicly. Anthropic said the release was "made possible by new safeguards that block responses in specific high-risk areas," with …

title: (No AI) Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A collection of AI generated or manipulated images showcased at WWDC 2026.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/Apple-generative-AI-photo-editing-lede.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Apple’s feature showcase at WWDC 2026 didn’t flag which if these “photographs” are real or created with its new AI fakery. | Images by Apple / compiled by _The Verge_

Apple [used to question](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fr8ALcEiYAk&t=471s) whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should accurately capture reality. At [WWDC 2026](https://www.theverge.com/tech/944110/wwdc-2026-news-announcements), the company announced a host of new [AI-powered photo editing tools](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/06/apple-intelligence-brings-powerful-ai-capabilities-into-everyday-experiences/). They give users effortless powers of manipulating images that Apple still refers to as "photos."

Two years ago, Apple [launched Clean Up](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/28/24272995/apple-intelligence-now-available-ios-18-1-mac-ipad) - an AI-powered object removal tool in Apple's Photo app that's similar to the [Magic Eraser](https://www.theverge.com/2023/2/23/23612034/google-photos-magic-eraser-pixel-iphone-one-subscription) feature in Google Photos. At the time, Apple software chief [Craig Federighi said](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/23/24277489/apple-intelligence-iphone-ai-photo-editing-craig-federighi-interview) that it was important for the company …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/946850/apple-ai-photo-editing-tools-ios27-wwdc-2026-deepfakes)
contentSnippet:
Apple’s feature showcase at WWDC 2026 didn’t flag which if these “photographs” are real or created with its new AI fakery. | Images by Apple / compiled by The Verge	

Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should accurately capture reality. At WWDC 2026, the company announced a host of new AI-powered photo editing tools. They give users effortless powers of manipulating images that Apple still refers to as "photos." 
Two years ago, Apple launched Clean Up - an AI-powered object removal tool in Apple's Photo app that's similar to the Magic Eraser feature in Google Photos. At the time, Apple software chief Craig Federighi said that it was important for the company …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Dell Cameron
title: (No AI) Wrongful Arrest Exposes Failures in One of the Oldest Police Face-Recognition Tools in the US
dc:creator: Dell Cameron
content: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.
contentSnippet: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.

creator: Fernanda González
title: (No AI) China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
dc:creator: Fernanda González
content: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
contentSnippet: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
